title: Erythropalum vagum
intro: Erythropalum vagum is a species of flowering plant in the family Opiliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Erythropalum vagum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Erythropalum vagum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Erythropalum vagum is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant is classified in the genus Erythropalum, which belongs to the order Magnoliales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Annonaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. Erythropalum vagum is a member of this diverse and widespread family of plants.
